CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-11-2018 Continued To Nov-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Advanced From Mar-14-18 To Mar-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Advanced From Mar-14-18 To Mar-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Request For Stay; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Roger Barton, Esq. and Jason Cohen, Esq. (through Court Call) 212-687-6262 of Barton for Plaintiff Dr. Harrison.
Bernard Resser, Esq. 310-553-3610 of Greenberg Glusker for Plaintiff Dr. Harrison.
Mark McKane, Esq. 415-439-1473 of Kirkland & Ellis LLP for Defendants Gryphon.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues equitable estoppel. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GRYPHON INVESTORS LLC., GRYPHON INVESTORS INC., GRYPHON ADVISORS, LLC., GRYPHON PARTNERS III LP., GRYPHON PARTNERS III-A LP, GRYPHON PARTNERS III-B, LP, GRYPHON CO-INVEST FUND III LP, GRYPHON PARTNERS III-C ANNEX FUND, LP, GRYPHON GENPAR III LP, R ANDREWS, ALEXANDER EARLS' Motion To Compel Arbitration And Request For Stay.

Defendants' motion to compel arbitration is granted and this case is stayed pending the completion of arbitration proceedings on the claims alleged by plaintiff Francisco Harrison. The doctrine of equitable estoppel requires the arbitration of all of Dr. Harrison's claims against all of the Gryphon entity and individual defendants "[A] party who has not signed a contract containing an arbitration clause may nonetheless be compelled to arbitrate when he seeks enforcement of other provisions of the same contract that benefit him." (Metalclad Corporation v. Ventana Environmental Organizational Partnership (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 1705, 1713.) It is proper to compel arbitration between a nonsignatory and signatory where the signatory's claims are "intimately founded in and intertwined with" the underlying agreement (Metalclad, 109 Cal.App.4th at 1713) and where the signatory uses "the substantive terms of those agreements as a foundation for ' [his] claims." (Goldman v. KPMG LLP (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 209, 221.) The Sale and Purchase Agreement between Dr. Harrison and Synteract includes a broad arbitration clause providing that "[a]ll disputes arising in connection with this Agreement or its validity" shall be arbitrated in Germany. Dr. Harrison's claims cannot be divorced from the SPA. The complaint is replete with references to the SPA and Dr. Harrison's claimed damages are based on that agreement. Goldman is factually distinguishable because the plaintiffs' complaints in that case did "not rely on or use any terms or obligations of the operating agreements as a foundation for their claims; indeed, the operating agreements are not even mentioned in the complaints." (Goldman, 173 Cal.App.4th at 218.) The federal authorities cited by Dr. Harrison are both non-binding and readily distinguishable. The collateral stock agreements that are unrelated to Dr. Harrison's claims do have any bearing on the application of equitable estoppel. Metalclad is binding on-point still valid authority that requires the granting of this motion.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Roger Barton, Esq. and Jason Cohen, Esq. (through Court Call) 212-687-6262 of Barton for Plaintiff Dr. Harrison.
Bernard Resser, Esq. 310-553-3610 of Greenberg Glusker for Plaintiff Dr. Harrison.
Mark McKane, Esq. 415-439-1473 of Kirkland & Ellis LLP for Defendants Gryphon.

RULING - The matter is Unopposed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FRANCISCO HARRISON'S MOTION AND VERIFIED APPLICATION OF ROGER E. BARTON TO APPEAR AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown.

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)
